description_long: This is a compact, utilitarian submachine gun with a tubular metal receiver and a simple, skeletonized wire stock. The weapon features a short, cylindrical barrel and a prominent magazine well beneath the receiver, with a detachable box magazine. The finish is matte black, giving it a non-reflective, tactical appearance suitable for urban environments. The stock is a single bent metal rod, designed for lightweight portability and rapid deployment. Iron sights are integrated into the top of the receiver, and the overall design is minimalistic, prioritizing function over aesthetics. Two small cartridges are visible in one view, indicating the ammunition type used.
description: A compact, matte black submachine gun with a tubular receiver, skeletonized wire stock, and detachable box magazine. Designed for utility and rapid use in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cylindrical barrel and the end of the wire stock, with the magazine protruding below.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the full length of the tubular receiver, wire stock, and the magazine well.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the wire stock and the back of the receiver, with the barrel and magazine visible from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the wire stock, receiver, and two cartridges placed below the barrel.
